ops.text: E44 detector-fires labeling: two failed launches root-caused en route — (1) spawnllm stdin race: claude --print's 3s stdin deadline vs post-spawn pipe write under a saturated event loop; FIXED in spawnllm (file-backed stdin, deterministic loop-jam regression tests, 12-way burst clean, shipped green) — also explains the 888 corpus-pass failures during the load-144 window; (2) ccp env routed to acct-19 (Yasyf-12) which has NO credential (1,000 rows failed "Not logged in", $0) — run_all.sh now takes E44_CCP_ACCOUNT pin. Pinned pass on acct-08 then hit the REAL constraint: per-account Fable-5 weekly quota — 318 judged ($79, $0.248/row) then 682 uniform "Fable 5 limit" failures; ccp status "Fable NN%" decodes as fable-quota-used and nearly all credentialed accounts are 80-98% depleted; the 18%-used account is exactly the uncredentialed acct-19. YIELD (n=351 labeled fires): only 43% fable-confirmed steering (152 pos: direction 98, wrong_approach 33, incorrect_change 13) — detector fires are NOT positives without confirmation; 199 fable-rejected fires = the first HARD NEGATIVES (the minority-class bottleneck). Frame inventory now: ~150+ new pos, 199 hard + 1,191 structural + 293 legacy negatives → projected MDE under the 0.02 target already. Frame rebuild proceeds once human-gold lands (board live, awaiting clicks); remaining 5,310 detector-fires + 4,883 corpus remainder are CAPACITY-gated: unlock = user runs `ccp login 19` (also 4, 5), or next week's quota. Total campaign fable spend so far ≈ $230.
